title: Podman on windows normalizes/replaces a backslashes and colons in environment variables
body:
### Issue Description

Podman replaces contents of environment variables; more specifically backslashes and colors. It seems like it tries to normalize paths/URLs, which would be incorrect. In certain circumstances, one will have to pass a value that may look like an incorrect path/URL, but which should remain unchanged.

A good example is an [SQL server JDBC URL](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/connect/jdbc/building-the-connection-url?view=sql-server-ver16), which mixes colons, semicolons, slashes and backslashes.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

`podman run -it --rm -e CONNURL="jdbc://abc\\;" alpine env`
`podman run -it --rm -e CONNURL=":/abc\\;" alpine env`

### Describe the results you received

...
`CONNURL=jdbc://abc/;` _Backslash is lost_
...
`CONNURL=;\abc\;` _Forward slash and colon are lost_
...

### Describe the results you expected

...
`CONNURL=jdbc://abc\;`
...
`CONNURL=:/abc\;`
...

### podman info output

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

This shows we are getting the argument correctly so it is not the shell changing it.
```
...podman.exe run --log-level debug -it --rm -e CONNURL=;\\abc\\; alpine env)"
```
I don't find any code where would ever try to change a env var value.
@n1hility Any idea?

I might have another clue:

It seems like the issue occurs in MSYS2 (using the shells Fish or Bash) and in Git Bash (using Bash), but the issue _does not occur_ in powershell.
In other words, it would seem like Podman behaves differently, depending on the Shell it's being invoked from.

`podman run --log-level debug -it --rm -e CONNURL=":/abc\\;" alpine env`

It's easy to miss but there is a difference here. // is becoming \ , so this is definitely the shell doing it

> `podman run --log-level debug -it --rm -e CONNURL=":/abc\\;" alpine env`
>  podman.exe run --log-level debug -it --rm -e CONNURL=;\\abc\\; alpine env)"

Msys and cygwin do automatic path conversion between unix style paths to windows style paths when a windows executable is ran.

You can work around this by setting the env var when you run your command: 
MSYS2_ARG_CONV_EXCL='*'

See https://www.msys2.org/docs/filesystem-paths/
